channel.title: Math Learning Site – Practice Questions
channel.description: Recent content in Practice Questions on Math Learning Site

item.title: One Step Equations
item.description:
Practice solving one-step linear equations using inverse operations.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \(8x = 4\)

Solve: \(x + 5 = 5\)

In the equation -3x = 12, why do we divide by -3 rather than adding 3?

Solve: \(0.5x = 10\)

Solve: \(x/10 = 0.1\)

In the equation x - 15 = 30, which inverse operation should you use to isolate x?

Solve: \(x / 4 = -2\)

Solve: \(x - 4 = -12\)

To solve (x / 5) = 10, you must multiply both sides by 5. What is the value of x?

item.title: Two Step Equations
item.description:
Practice solving two-step linear equations.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \(10x - 3 = 7\)

Solve: \(3x + 5 = 20\)

Solve: \(x/3 + 1 = 4\)

Solve: \(12 = 3x - 6\)

Solve: \(4x + 1 = 13\)

Solve: \(4 - 3x = -2\)

Given 4x + 8 = 20, what is the best first step to solve for x?

Solve: \(x/2 - 5 = -2\)

Solve for x: \(3x - 5 = 10\)

Solve: \(x + 0.1 = 0.5\)

Solve: \(-x + 4 = 10\)

Solve: \(2x + 7 = 1\)

Solve: \(5x - 10 = 0\)

item.title: Multi Step Equations
item.description:
Practice solving multi-step linear equations.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \(3(x + 2) = 12\)

Solve for x: \(2x + 5x - 3 = 11\)

Solve: \(2x/5 = 4\)

Solve: \(2(x - 3) = 10\)

Solve: \(x + x + x = 18\)

Solve: \(15 = 5(x - 2)\)

When solving 3(x - 4) = 15, what does the equation look like after applying the distributive property?

Solve for x: \((2/3)x + 4 = 10\)

Solve: \(5(x - 1) = 20\)

item.title: Variables on Both Sides
item.description:
Practice solving equations with variables on both sides.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \(6x - 2 = 4x + 8\)

Solve: \(4x + 2 = 2x + 10\)

Solve: \(2x + 5 = 2x + 5\)

Solve for x: \(5x + 3 = 2x + 12\)

Solve: \(4(x + 1) = 4x + 4\)

Solve: \(7x = 2x + 25\)

Solve: \(2x + 1 = 3x - 5\)

Solve: \(3x + 3 = 3(x + 1)\)

Solve: \(3x - 4 = 3x + 10\)

item.title: Substitution
item.description:
Practice solving systems of linear equations using substitution.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \(3x - 2y = 12 and y = 0\)

Solve: \(x = 4 and x + y = 10\)

For the system { y = 4x - 1 ; 2x + 3y = 10 }, which method is most efficient to use first?

Solve using substitution: \(y = 2x and x + y = 9.\)

Solve: \(y = 4x and x + y = 5\)

If you are solving { x = y + 2 ; 3x + y = 10 } by substitution, what should the second equation look like after the first step?

Solve: \(2x + 3y = 6 and x = 0\)

Solve: \(2x + y = 7 and y = 3\)

Solve: \(y = -x + 4 and y = 2x - 2\)

Solve: \(y = 2x - 7 and y = -x + 2\)

Solve: \(y = x + 1 and y = -x + 5\)

Solve: \(10x + y = 25 and y = 5\)

Solve: \(3x + 2y = 12 and y = x - 4\)

Solve: \(3x + y = 15 and y = 2x\)

Solve: \(x + 2y = 8 and x = 2y\)

Solve: \(y = x and 2x + y = 9\)

Solve: \(4x - 3y = 1 and x = y\)

Solve: \(y = 3x - 5 and y = 2x + 1\)

Solve: \(3x + 2y = 13 and y = 2\)

item.title: Elimination
item.description:
Practice solving systems of linear equations using elimination.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Solve: \(4x + y = 11 and x + y = 5\)

Solve: \(2x + y = 10 and x - y = -1\)

Solve: \(x + y = 12 and x - y = 2\)

Solve: \(x + y = 7 and 2x + y = 9\)

Solve: \(5x - 2y = 4 and x + y = 5\)

Solve: \(x + y = 8 and x - y = 8\)

Solve: \(2x + 2y = 20 and x - y = 2\)

Solve: \(2x - y = 8 and x + y = 1\)

Solve: \(x - y = 5 and x + y = 15\)

Solve: \(2x + 4y = 8 and x + y = 3\)

Solve: \(x + 3y = 7 and x + y = 3\)

Solve: \(x + y = 0 and x - y = 10\)

Solve using elimination: \(x + y = 10 and x - y = 2.\)

Solve: \(2x + 3y = 12 and x - y = 1.\)

Solve: \(2x - 3y = -1 and x + y = 2\)

Solve: \(3x - y = 7 and 2x + y = 8\)

Solve: \(4x + 2y = 10 and 3x - 2y = 4\)

Solve: \(x + y = 100 and x - y = 20\)

Solve: \(5x + y = 21 and x + y = 5\)

Solve: \(2x + y = 10 and 2x - y = 2\)

To solve the system { 3x + y = 10 ; 2x - y = 5 } using elimination, what is the most logical first step?

item.title: Word Problems
item.description:
Practice modeling and solving systems of linear equations in word problems.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

A plumber charges $50 for a visit plus $30 per hour. Which equation represents total cost C for h hours?

The sum of two numbers is 20 and their difference is 4. Find the larger number.

Company A charges $20 + $2/mile. Company B charges $10 + $3/mile. At how many miles is the cost identical?

item.title: Graphing Linear Equations
item.description:
Practice reading and matching linear graphs, including graph-in-question and graph-as-answer formats.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Which graph matches a line through (0, 4) and (2, 0)?

Which graph matches y = 4x?

What is the value of b in y = mx + b for the graph shown?

Which point lies on the line shown?

Which equation matches the vertical line shown?

Which graph has x-intercept -3 and y-intercept 3?

Which equation matches the graph?

Which graph shows an undefined slope at x = 4?

Which graph matches a line with slope -1 and y-intercept -2?

Which graph matches y = -3?

What is the slope of the line shown?

What is the y-intercept of the graph?

What is the y-intercept of the line shown on the graph?

Which graph matches a line with slope 3/2 and y-intercept -4?

What is the x-intercept of the graph?

Which statement correctly compares the intercepts of the graph?

The graph shows the cost c of a gym pass after x visits. What does the c-intercept represent?

Which equation is represented by the graph?

A line decreases 2 units for every 3 units x increases and crosses the y-axis at 4. Which graph matches it?

What is the slope of the line on the graph?

Which graph matches 2x - y = -6?

Which equation matches the line shown?

Which graph represents 4x - 2y = 8?

Which table could represent the line shown?

Which graph matches x + y = 4?

Which statement about the line is true?

Which graph passes through (0, -1) and (3, 2)?

Which equation matches the graph?

Which graph matches x + 2y = 6?

Which graph represents 3x + y = 3?

What is the y-intercept of the line shown?

Which graph shows x = -1?

Which graph shows the constant function y = 5?

Which graph matches y = -2/3x + 2?

Which graph represents y = -1/2x - 2?

Which equation matches the graph?

Which graph shows the constant function y = 3?

Which graph represents y = -x + 4?

Which equation matches the horizontal line shown?

Which graph shows the vertical line x = 2?

How would you describe the slope of this line?

A function increases 1 unit for every 5 units x decreases, and its value at 0 is 3. Which graph matches it?

What is the x-intercept of the line shown?

Which graph represents y = 2x + 1?

Which graph matches y = x - 5?

The graph shows the height h of a drone after t minutes. What does the slope represent?

What is the slope of the line through the plotted points on the graph?

Which graph is parallel to y = -2x + 5 and has y-intercept -3?

A linear function increases 4 units for every 2 units x increases and has value -1 when x = 0. Which graph matches it?

What is the slope of the line on the graph?

item.title: Miscellaneous
item.description:
Practice a mixed set of linear algebra questions.

Practice Question Set

7 quick practice checks

Question 1 of 7

Find the slope perpendicular to a horizontal line.

Slope between (2, 1) and (4, 2)?

Equation of a line perpendicular to y = 3x through (0,0).

Slope of a line through the origin and (2, 4)?

Find the slope of y = x.

Find slope: \((10, 5) and (12, 4).\)

Convert 15x + 3y = 9 to slope-intercept form.

Y-intercept of x + y = 10?

A student says y = 2x + 5 and y = -2x + 3 are perpendicular. What is their mistake?

A student calculates slope between (1, 2) and (3, 10) as (3 - 1) / (10 - 2) = 2/8. What was the error?

To convert 3x + y = 10 into slope-intercept form, what is the first algebraic step?

Find the y-intercept of 2x + 5y = 20.

Line perpendicular to y = 1/5x + 2.

A student converts y - 2 = 3(x - 1) to y = 3x - 1. What was their mistake?

Are y = 0.5x and y = -2x perpendicular?

Calculate the slope between (-1/2, 2) and (1/2, 5).

If a line has a slope of 4, what is the slope of a line that is perpendicular to it?

Find the x-intercept of 3x - y = 9.

X-intercept of 3x + y = 9?

Are 2x + y = 4 and y = -2x + 8 parallel?

Slope between (1, 1) and (2, 2)?

Standard form of y - 3 = 0.5(x + 4).

X-intercept of 5x + 10y = 50?

Find the slope of 3x - 4y = 12.

In the point-slope form y - y1 = m(x - x1), what does the 'm' represent?

The y-intercept of a line is the point where the graph crosses the y-axis. At this point, what is always the value of x?

Which line is perpendicular to y = 0 (the x-axis)?

Find the slope of a line passing through (4, 7) and (4, -2).

Convert y - 2 = 4(x - 1) into Standard Form (Ax + By = C).

A system has one solution. If the first line has a slope of 3, which of the following MUST be true about the second line?

Convert 2x - y = 5 into slope-intercept form (y = mx + b).

The slope of Line J is -2/7. To find the perpendicular slope, you flip the fraction and change the sign. What is the result?

Find the slope of -2x + 8y = 16.

Which line is perpendicular to y = -2x + 1?

Convert y - 5 = 4(x + 2) to Standard Form.

If two lines have the same slope AND the same y-intercept, which of the following is true?

Write the equation in y = mx + b form for points (0, 3) and (2, 7).

Point-slope form of a line with m=4 through (2, 2).

What is the x-intercept of the line x + y = 0?

Are 4x - y = 2 and y = 4x + 7 parallel?

Slope of a line perpendicular to y = x.

Line parallel to y = 10x - 1.

Slope perpendicular to m = 1/3.

Slope of 12x + 4y = 24.

Standard form of y = -4/5x + 2.

In the equation y = 4x - 7, what is the y-intercept?

Are y = 3x + 4 and 6x - 2y = 10 parallel?

What is the slope of the line passing through (5, -3) and (2, -3)?

Which of the following is perpendicular to the vertical line x = -4?

What is the slope of the x-axis?

A line with an undefined slope passes through (7, -1). What is its x-intercept?

Perpendicular lines meet at a 90-degree angle. Their slopes are 'negative reciprocals,' meaning if one slope is a/b, the other is

Convert y = -x + 6 to Standard Form.

Slope of y = 10?

Find the x-intercept of 2x + 4y = 8.

If y = 3, what is the slope?

Convert 3x - 3y = 9 to slope-intercept form.

Convert 10x - 2y = 8 to slope-intercept form.

What is the y-intercept of y = 12x?

Y-intercept of 4x + 2y = 12?

If the slope of a line is positive, and the y-intercept is negative, in which quadrant will the x-intercept be located?

Find the y-intercept of the line 5x - 2y = 10.

Which form is most useful for identifying the y-intercept at a glance?

For the horizontal line y = -3, what is the x-intercept?

From the equation y - 5 = -3(x + 2), which specific point is the line known to pass through?

If a line has a slope of 2/3, what is the slope of a perpendicular line?

Find slope: \((-1, -1) and (1, 1).\)

What is the slope of 5x + 2y = 20?

Find the equation of a line parallel to y = 4x + 1 through (0, -3).

Y-intercept of y = 0.5x + 3?

Is y = 5 parallel to y = -5?

Which line is perpendicular to x = 4?

Slope of a line perpendicular to 3x + y = 5.

If you have y - 4 = 2(x - 3), what is the resulting equation in slope-intercept form after simplifying?

Which equation format is known as Slope-Intercept form?

Find a line parallel to y = 2x - 3 passing through the origin (0,0).

Slope perpendicular to m = 5/6.

What is the slope of 0.2x - 0.2y = 1?

What is the y-intercept of y + 2 = 3(x - 1)?

Convert 2x - y = 5 to slope-intercept form.

Solve the formula for h: \(V = πr²h\)

Y-intercept of y = -x - 4?

Point-slope through (-1, -1) with m = 5.

X-intercept of y = x - 5?

What is the slope of a vertical line like x = 5?

Find line parallel to y = -x + 4 through (0, 5).

Write an equation in point-slope form for a line with m = -1 through (0, 0).

Perpendicular slope to m = -1.

If a line goes 'downhill' from left to right, what kind of slope does it have?

Find the equation of a line parallel to y = 4x + 1 that passes through (0, -3).

Parallel slope to m = 0.75.

The solution to a system of equations on a graph is located at the ________ of the two lines.

Solve for y in terms of x: \(2x + y = 10\)

When two lines in a system are parallel and never touch, how many solutions does the system have?

The formula for perimeter is P = 2L + 2W. Solve for W.

X-intercept of 2x - y = 4?

Are x = 2 and x = 5 parallel?

What is the slope of y = -3?

Convert 7x + y = 14 to slope-intercept form.

On a graph, Line 1 rises 3 units for every 2 units it moves right. Line 2 also rises 3 units for every 2 units it moves right. These lines are

A student solves a system and gets '0 = 5'. What does this result indicate?

Solve: \(3x + 4 = 3x - 2\)

Two lines are parallel if they are in the same plane and never intersect. Mathematically, this means their slopes must be

Slope of a line parallel to 2x - 5y = 10.

In the equation y = -4x + 7, what is the y-intercept?

Write the Standard Form of a line where m = 2 and the y-intercept is 3.

A plumber charges $50 for a visit plus $30 per hour. Which equation represents the total cost (C) for (h) hours?

Which line is parallel to x = 0 (the y-axis)?

A line that is perfectly flat (horizontal) has which type of slope?

What is the slope of the line represented by the equation 5x + 2y = 10?

If a line has a positive x-intercept and a positive y-intercept, which direction must the slope be?

Solve for m: \(y = mx + b\)

Which point is the solution to the system { x - y = 4 ; x + y = 10 }?

Calculate the slope of the line passing through (-2, 4) and (1, 10).

Slope perpendicular to m = 10.

Line A has a slope of -5. If Line B is parallel to Line A, what is its slope?

What is the slope of the line 3x + 2y = 10?

Solve for a: \(F = ma\)

Convert y - 10 = -1(x + 5) to slope-intercept.

What is the constant 'C' in the standard form of y = 2x + 5?

If a line passes through the origin (0, 0), what are its intercepts?

Slope of a line parallel to y = -1/2x + 8.

Solve: \(2(x + 3) = 2x + 6\)

If a line has a slope of 0 and passes through (2, 5), what is its equation?

Write in slope-intercept form: \(m = 1/2, through (0, 5).\)

In the equation Ax + By = C, what is this specific form called?

Is (2, 3) a solution to the system: \(x + y = 5 and 2x - y = 1?\)

Convert y = 2/3x - 1 to Standard Form (no fractions).

What is the slope of x = 10?

Which point lies on y - 1 = 2(x + 3)?

For the vertical line x = 4, what is the y-intercept?

Are y = x + 2 and y = -x + 2 perpendicular?

Find the slope: \((0, 0) and (5, 5).\)

Convert y = 3x - 4 to Standard Form.

If two equations in a system represent the exact same line, the system is said to have

Convert y + 1 = -2(x - 4) to slope-intercept form.

Find the slope of the line: \(y + 8 = -5(x - 2).\)

If two lines in a system are parallel, how many solutions exist?

Find the x-intercept of the line y = 3x - 9.

Which equation represents a line with slope 3 passing through (1, 4)?

Solve: \(10 - x = 15\)

Slope between (-5, 2) and (-5, 8)?

Perpendicular slope to m = -2/3.

Are y = 2x + 1 and y = 2x - 1 parallel?

Does the point (1, 5) satisfy the equation y = 2x + 3?

In solving x + 7 = -3, a student gets x = 4. What did they do wrong?

What is the x-intercept of x = -2?

Solve: \(1 - 2x = 9\)

Slope parallel to m = -2/9.

Line parallel to y = -4x + 1.

Solve for r: \(C = 2πr\)

Find the slope (m) between (1, 2) and (3, 6).

Slope between (4, 3) and (8, 3)?

In the Standard Form Ax + By = C, which of the following is usually a requirement for the coefficient A?

Find the perpendicular slope to m = -0.1.

Slope of a line through (1, 1) and (2, 3) in y=mx+b form.

Which equation is in slope-intercept form?

Parallel slope to m = 1.

Which represents the same line as y = 2x - 4?

Parallel lines must have the same ______.

In the context of a graph, slope is often described as the ratio of the vertical change to the horizontal change. What is the common nickname for this?

Convert 4x + 6y = 12 to slope-intercept form.

Are the lines 2x + 3y = 6 and 3x - 2y = 8 perpendicular?

X-intercept of y = 2x + 10?

Identify the point and slope from: \(y + 2 = 0.5(x - 7)\)

Which slope is perpendicular to m = -3/4?

Identify form: \(y = 10.\)

Calculate the y-intercept for the line 0.5x + 0.1y = 1.

Slope of x = -100?

The x-intercept of a line is the point where the graph crosses the x-axis. At this point, what is always the value of y?

Which form is Ax + By = C?

Y-intercept of y = 3x - 1?

Perpendicular slope to m = 1/8.

A line with a slope of 0 (horizontal) is always perpendicular to a line with what kind of slope?

A student solves 4x = 2 and says x = 2. What was the error?

When you multiply the slopes of two perpendicular lines (that are not vertical or horizontal), the product is always

A line that goes straight up and down (vertical) has which type of slope?

Solve for x: \(2(x + 4) = 18\)

Which type of line is parallel to the horizontal line y = 10?
